mechanism: No computational core. Empirical study testing whether the Stroop effect modification arises from modulation of attention based on stimulus properties or from learned stimulus-response contingencies. Tests these competing theoretical accounts through reanalysis of existing data and new experiments examining response patterns and their interactions with stimulus presentation frequencies.

No computational core. Empirical demonstration of a behavioral dilemma in probabilistic inference: two distinct information search strategies (natural sampling of relevant outcomes; deliberate sampling of raw data) both introduce different systematic biases depending on the inference question, making neither strategy universally optimal. Demonstrates through simulation and experiments that searchers face an inescapable trade-off.

No computational core. Empirical study examining recognition memory through serial recall patterns across stimulus positions and origins. Tests dual-process theory (familiarity and recollection) with two retrieval routes (context-to-item and item-to-context) against single-process alternatives by analyzing whether recognition curves remain parallel across experimental manipulations.

No computational core. Empirical test of whether directed forgetting (reduced recall of items in a to-be-forgotten list) depends on context strength or item strength. Manipulates memory strength through spaced repetition versus processing time, comparing forgetting magnitudes, to adjudicate between competing theoretical accounts of how forgetting is driven.

No computational core. Empirical comparison of inhibitory control mechanisms in monolinguals versus bilinguals across three behavioral tasks (stop signal, inhibition of return, attentional blink) that tap different inhibition aspects. Tests whether bilingual advantage in inhibition is active (direct suppression) or reactive (goal-maintenance and bias), determining which inhibitory mechanism differs across groups.

No computational core. Empirical study examining automatic priming of arithmetic facts in a word-priming task. Tests whether activation of addition facts (e.g., 5+3=8) is automatically triggered or depends on semantic alignment between prime words and the arithmetic structure, demonstrating obligatory analogical coordination between semantic and arithmetic relations.

No computational core. Empirical demonstration of implicit learning through two manifestations of the Hebb repetition effect in serial recall: improvement in correct recalls for a repeated sequence over a session, and increased probability of responses (correct or incorrect) that occurred previously. Characterizes learning as strengthening any response that was previously produced, regardless of accuracy.

Analysis of response-time distributions in lexical decision to uncover underlying processing interactions. Given stimulus quality and word frequency inputs, decompose the response-time distribution to reveal when mean additivity masks interaction in distribution tails. When familiarity is viable, discrete processing applies; when deemphasized, cascaded letter-to-word processing applies. Method: estimate distributional shape parameters (quantiles, skew) and test for interaction at different distribution positions.

No computational core. Empirical test using picture-word interference to dissociate loci of semantic and frequency effects in naming. Manipulates distractor timing (simultaneous vs. delayed) to determine whether semantic interference arises at lexical selection (requires immediate naming) or postlexical output stages (visible in delayed naming). Tests competing mechanistic accounts through response-latency patterns.

No computational core. Empirical study determining the scope of visual statistical learning. Tests whether learners extract regularities over individual feature dimensions independently or bind dimensions into multidimensional objects. Shows learning scope depends on feature covariance during encoding: perfect covariance yields object-based learning; partial decoupling yields feature-based learning, revealing implicit discovery of task-relevant structure.

Weighted averaging model for causal judgment from contingency information. Given two potential causes, compute each cause's causal strength from: (1) conditional probability of cause given outcome (presence when outcome occurs), and (2) marginal rate of outcome given that cause alone is present. Combine these components via weighted averaging to predict scalar causal judgment. Model explains interactions: overshadowing weakens, causal strength of unmatched cause increases, capacity to account for occurrences modulates judgment value.

No computational core. Empirical study of short-term memory for temporal and order information through serial recall tasks with irregular stimulus spacing. Tests whether order memory is driven by a timing signal by examining effects of grouping instructions, correspondence between input timing and recall timing, and relative isolation of items. Constrains timing-based theories of positional coding.

No computational core. Empirical study testing whether learning of dependencies (adjacent and nonadjacent relations) in stimulus materials requires active attention to those relations. Demonstrates that when task demands specify which relations to attend, learners acquire only those relations irrespective of spatial contiguity, supporting the hypothesis that joint attentional processing is both necessary and sufficient for learning.

No computational core. Empirical study of visual working memory capacity via change detection with retro-cues (post-encoding, pre-test cues). Tests whether retro-cue benefit reflects memory decay reduction or interference reduction. Shows cuing an item 1 second after encoding enhances detection of changes to that item, attributed to attentional focus making memory contents resistant to new interference rather than preventing decay.

Bayesian sequential decision model over stochastic environments. Compute posterior belief distribution on environment parameters (outcome probabilities) via Bayesian updating with observed samples. In each decision step, compute expected utility under current belief and select highest-utility action (explore or exploit given risk-return tradeoff). Model extends across multiple task structures; identifies that Bayesian learning process and decision process can interfere or facilitate task-specific inference diagnosticity depending on environment statistics.

No computational core. Empirical demonstration of rejection of moral rebels through four studies varying participant role (actor vs. observer) and task involvement (racist task vs. stated-values condition). Shows participants dislike rebels who refuse unethical tasks but observers do not; effect mediated by inference that rebel rejects conforming participants; prevented by self-affirming value statements. Measures attitudes and inferences, does not propose computational process.

No computational core. Empirical study examining why implicit and explicit attitude tests show weak correlation by testing the hypothesis that test correlations depend on structural similarity in task demands. Uses affect misattribution procedure with varied structural fit; correlations increase with format similarity, suggesting weak correlations reflect measurement differences rather than independent implicit/explicit cognition. Tests theoretical claims about cognition through correlation patterns.

No computational core. Empirical psychometric analysis of Big Five personality self-reports across ages 10-20. Decomposes age-related variance into acquiescence effects, within-domain coherence (consistency), and across-domain differentiation (independence). Documents developmental trends: acquiescence declines, coherence and differentiation increase, with domain-specific patterns. Analyzes factorial structure and comprehension effects to exclude confounds.

No computational core. Empirical study of attachment as a terror management strategy. Six studies examine whether activating parental attachment after mortality salience reduces death anxiety and defensive worldview responses, with effects moderated by attachment style. Shows insecure individuals rely on parent attachment after mortality salience; secure individuals rely on romantic partners. Tests theoretical claims through reaction time and attraction measures.

Model of general action and inaction goals influencing motor and cognitive output magnitude. Input: contextual priming or goal instruction specifying action or inaction tendency. Output: magnitude of observable behavior (doodling intensity, eating quantity) or cognitive productivity (recall, problem solutions). Mechanism: primes instill goals to achieve active or inactive states; goal attainment decreases sensitivity to continued priming. System-neutral: same circuitry and goal dynamics govern diverse motor and cognitive behaviors. Tested through prime effects and subsequent goal-attainment decay.
